title: Leisure battery is not charging
safety: Disconnect 230 V before opening electrical enclosures.

steps.q: Is the vehicle connected to a known-working 230 V hook-up?
steps.no: Connect to a verified supply and confirm the site breaker and vehicle RCD are on.

steps.q: Does the control panel show mains present?
steps.no: Check the hook-up lead, inlet, RCD and charger mains supply.

steps.q: Is the leisure battery above 11.5 V at rest?
steps.no: The battery may be deeply discharged or failed. Charge externally only if safe and compatible.

steps.q: Does battery voltage rise when mains charging is active?
steps.yes: Charging is operating. Investigate battery capacity, parasitic loads or poor connections.

steps.q: Are charger and battery fuses intact and terminals tight?
steps.yes: Likely charger, relay, wiring or battery fault. Arrange targeted electrical testing.
steps.no: Replace only with the specified fuse rating or clean and tighten the connection, then retest.

steps.q: Is the battery fully charged and known to be serviceable?
steps.no: Fully charge and test the battery before looking for a vehicle drain.

steps.q: Are obvious loads such as heating fan, lights, inverter or fridge left on?
steps.yes: Switch off the load and monitor battery voltage.

steps.q: Does the voltage still fall rapidly with the habitation master switch off?
steps.no: A normal habitation load is responsible; isolate circuits one at a time.

steps.q: Does removing one habitation fuse stop the drain?
steps.yes: The drain is on that circuit. Inspect its appliances and wiring.
steps.no: Check battery condition, direct battery connections and charging performance.

title: 230 V hook-up is not working
safety: 230 V AC can kill. Do not remove covers unless competent.
Use only tested hook-up leads and adapters.

steps.q: Does the supply post show power and its breaker remain on?
steps.no: Reset or report the site supply. Test with another outlet if permitted.

steps.q: Does a known-good hook-up lead work?
steps.no: Replace the damaged or suspect lead.

steps.q: Is the vehicle RCD and main breaker on?
steps.no: Reset once. If it trips again, disconnect appliances and arrange inspection.

steps.q: Do any 230 V sockets work?
steps.yes: The fault is limited to one appliance or branch circuit.
steps.no: The inlet, RCD, consumer unit or internal wiring requires competent testing.

title: Habitation lights do not work
safety: Replace fuses only with the stated rating.

steps.q: Is the habitation master switch on and battery voltage normal?
steps.no: Switch on habitation power or charge the battery.

steps.q: Do all lights fail or only one fitting?
steps.no: Check the individual lamp, switch and local wiring.

steps.q: Is the lighting fuse intact?
steps.yes: Check the control panel output, distribution connector and earth return.
steps.no: Replace with the correct fuse rating and investigate if it blows again.

title: Water pump runs continuously
safety: Switch the pump off if no water is available.
Dry running can damage the pump.

steps.q: Is there enough water in the fresh tank?
steps.no: Fill the tank and prime the system.

steps.q: Is water visibly leaking from a tap, hose, boiler drain or underneath the vehicle?
steps.yes: Switch off the pump and repair the leak before further use.

steps.q: Does opening and closing each tap release trapped air?
steps.no: Prime the pump and inspect the suction side for air leaks.

steps.q: Does the pump stop after the system repressurises?
steps.yes: The issue was trapped air or incomplete priming.
steps.no: The pressure switch, non-return valve or hidden leak requires inspection.

title: Water pump will not run
safety: Do not bypass the pump fuse.

steps.q: Is the pump switch on and the habitation battery charged?
steps.no: Restore 12 V habitation power and turn the pump on.

steps.q: Is the pump fuse intact?
steps.no: Replace only with the specified rating and investigate if it fails again.

steps.q: Is 12 V present at the pump when water is demanded?
steps.yes: The pump or its local earth is likely faulty.
steps.no: Check switch, control panel output and wiring to the pump.

title: No hot water
safety: Never operate a boiler that is empty.
Stop if there is a gas smell or combustion warning.

steps.q: Is the boiler completely full of water?
steps.no: Fill and bleed the hot-water system before heating.

steps.q: Does another gas appliance work?
steps.no: Check cylinder, regulator and gas isolation first.

steps.q: Is the boiler receiving stable 12 V control power?
steps.no: Check the heating fuse, battery voltage and earth.

steps.q: Is the frost valve closed and the boiler retaining water?
steps.no: Warm the area, close the valve correctly and refill.

steps.q: Is a fault code displayed?
steps.yes: Record the exact code and use the appliance-specific service information.
steps.no: Inspect controls and flue, then arrange boiler service if it still fails.

steps.q: Does the hob light normally?
steps.no: Use the no-gas diagnostic first.

steps.q: Is the heater gas isolation valve open?
steps.no: Open the correct valve and retry according to the heater instructions.

steps.q: Is stable 12 V available and the heating fuse intact?
steps.no: Restore the 12 V supply and replace only the correct fuse.

steps.q: Is the exterior flue clear?
steps.no: Remove the obstruction before use.

steps.q: Is a fault code shown?
steps.yes: Record the exact code and follow the model-specific manual.
steps.no: Allow gas-line purge time, then arrange service if ignition still fails.

title: Refrigerator will not operate on gas
safety: Do not operate with blocked refrigerator vents.

steps.q: Does the hob operate normally?
steps.no: Check the cylinder, regulator and manifold.

steps.q: Is the refrigerator gas isolation valve open?
steps.no: Open the correct valve and retry.

steps.q: Are the exterior refrigerator vents clear?
steps.no: Clear them before use.

steps.q: Does ignition click or indicate an attempt?
steps.no: Check 12 V control power and ignition controls.

steps.q: Does the flame light but then go out?
steps.yes: Burner, thermocouple or flue servicing is likely required.
steps.no: Arrange burner and gas-path inspection.

title: Toilet flush does not work
safety: Use gloves around the cassette compartment.

steps.q: Is there water available for the flush system?
steps.no: Fill the flush reservoir or fresh-water system, depending on model.

steps.q: Is the toilet or pump fuse intact?
steps.no: Replace with the specified rating.

steps.q: Can the flush pump be heard?
steps.yes: Inspect the nozzle, hose and water supply for blockage.
steps.no: Check the switch, local connector, pump and earth.

title: Rear camera has no picture
safety: Secure the vehicle before working around reverse wiring.

steps.q: Does the monitor power on?
steps.no: Check monitor supply, fuse and earth.

steps.q: Does the system switch modes when reverse is selected?
steps.no: Check reverse-trigger signal or manual-view control.

steps.q: Is the image intermittent when wiring is moved gently?
steps.yes: Inspect connectors and video cable for poor contact.
steps.no: Check camera power at the rear and confirm the camera output.

title: Awning light does not work
safety: Use safe access equipment if reaching above shoulder height.

steps.q: Do other habitation lights work?
steps.no: Check habitation master power and lighting supply first.

steps.q: Is the awning-light switch supplying 12 V?
steps.no: Check the switch, fuse and feed.

steps.q: Is 12 V present at the light fitting?
steps.yes: Replace or repair the lamp fitting and verify its earth.
steps.no: Inspect wiring and connectors between the switch and fitting.
